commit.message:
Fix dashboard data loading, un-dismissable delete modal, and redesign dashboard/scenario-creation as SPA modals (#93)

The dashboard silently showed empty state on load failure, the delete
confirmation modal could not be dismissed, and scenario creation used a
confusing inline form inside the auth dropdown. Dashboard navigation
also caused a full page reload breaking SPA flow.

## Bug Fixes

### CSS modal visibility (root cause of un-dismissable delete modal)
`display: flex` on `.modal-overlay` overrides the browser's `[hidden]`
attribute style, making all modals permanently visible:
```css
/* Fix: author styles beat UA stylesheet [hidden] { display: none } */
.modal-overlay[hidden] { display: none; }
```

### `storageManager` never initialized in `app.js`
`cloudStorageAvailable` was always `false` in the main app context —
`createNewScenario()`, `listScenarios()` etc. silently returned
`null`/`[]`. Fixed by calling `storageManager.initialize()` after
successful auth check in `initializeAuth()`.

### `textContent` + `escapeHtml` double-encoding
`openDeleteModal` applied `escapeHtml()` to text assigned via
`.textContent`, producing visible `&` etc. for names with special
characters. Removed the escaping.

### Dashboard error state
`loadAndRenderScenarios()` caught all errors and fell through to the
empty state with no user feedback. Added explicit error tracking, a
visible error state in `dashboard.html`, and `[Dashboard]` prefixed
console warnings for auth/cloud misconfiguration.

## SPA Modal Architecture

### Dashboard as in-app overlay
- New `src/js/ui/dashboard-modal.js` — full scenario CRUD (list, rename,
delete, create) as an in-page overlay, initialising `storageManager` on
open if needed.
- Added `#dashboard-modal` (large overlay, max-width 900px, 90vh,
scrollable) plus inner `#dm-rename-modal` / `#dm-delete-modal` to
`index.html`.
- Converted "My Scenarios" `<a href="/dashboard">` → `<button
id="btn-my-scenarios">` that opens the overlay; no page reload.

### New Scenario as modal dialog
- Added `#new-scenario-modal` to `index.html`; removed the inline
`#new-scenario-form` from the auth dropdown.
- `wireNewScenarioModal()` (guarded against double-binding) wires
cancel/confirm/Escape/backdrop-click. Focus is restored to the trigger
element on close.

## Accessibility
All new modals include `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, labelled by
a visible heading, and restore focus to the triggering element on close.
`aria-label` values updated via `setAttribute` use raw text (not
`escapeHtml`) to avoid screen readers announcing `&`.

> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*

> <issue_description>## Problem
> - The dashboard page does not load data as expected.
> - The delete confirmation box on the dashboard is un-dismissable,
preventing users from proceeding.
> - Scenario creation currently uses a form in the menu, which confuses
users.
> 
> Dashboard data not loading — dashboard-manager.js calls
storageManager.listScenarios(), which silently returns [] if
cloudStorageAvailable is false or if the fetch fails. If the
storageManager.initialize() hasn't resolved auth/cloud availability
correctly before loadAndRenderScenarios() is called, the dashboard will
show the empty state with no error feedback to the user.
> Un-dismissable delete modal — The wireModalHandlers() in
dashboard-manager.js does wire up delete-cancel-btn, overlay click, and
Escape key to closeDeleteModal(). The bug is likely a CSS issue — the
modal-overlay may not be covering the full viewport or the hidden
attribute isn't being toggled correctly in certain states, making it
appear stuck.
> Scenario creation in the menu — In index.html/app.js, the "New
Scenario" button expands an inline form (#new-scenario-form) inside the
auth/settings panel dropdown. This is confusing as it sits inside a
small dropdown rather than a focused modal dialog.
> Dashboard as a separate route — The dashboard currently lives at
/dashboard as a separate HTML page (src/dashboard.html). The proposal is
to bring it inline as a large modal/panel overlay on the main SPA,
avoiding a full page navigation.
> 
> ## Proposed Solution
> - Fix the dashboard page to properly load and display data.
> - Convert the dashboard display to a modal popup covering the majority
of the SPA viewport (instead of a dedicated route/page), matching single
page architecture best practices.
> - Make the delete confirmation modal dismissable and ensure consistent
modal interaction patterns.
> - Change scenario creation to also use a modal popup (instead of the
in-menu form) to reduce confusion and unify the UI pattern.
> 
> ## Acceptance Criteria
> - Dashboard loads and displays data as expected.
> - Delete confirmation box is fully dismissable and does not block
usage.
> - Dashboard and scenario creation dialogs use modal popup UIs,
occupying most of the page as overlays.
> - UI/UX matches SPA expectations (no full page reload; overlays fit
visually with rest of app).
> - Improvements documented in docs/current_state/ and screenshots
provided for desktop/mobile/tablet.
> 
> ## Additional Notes
> - Consider accessibility (tab order, ARIA labels, focus restoration)
for all modals.
> - Update documentation and master_plan.md as per repo conventions.
> - Add/update visual documentation, especially before/after UI
screenshots.

> Add clear console logging for misconfigurations and issues

commit.message:
fix: CORS-safe image loading with retry fallback and diagnostics

Three bugs found via end-to-end asset pipeline audit:

1. removeBackground() threw SecurityError on cross-origin canvas
   getImageData() — prevented character sheets from loading AND
   blocked assetsReady flag. Now catches CORS errors and returns
   raw image as fallback.

2. All Image loaders (tileset metadata, legacy tileset, character
   sheets) now try crossOrigin='anonymous' first, then retry
   without CORS on failure. This handles VS Code webview
   environments that may not send Access-Control-Allow-Origin.

3. Fixed furnitureLoaded vs furnitureAssetsLoaded message name
   mismatch (extension sent one, webview handled the other).

Added comprehensive diagnostic logging throughout the pipeline
(extension host + webview) visible in VS Code Developer Tools
so the exact failure point can be identified at runtime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace Thread.delay with Unix.sleepf in eval for Domain.spawn compatibility (#12764)

* why...

* add sctx.pool

* Fix pool usage, enable parallel analyzer, fix data races

- Add Parallel.run_with_pool to wrap operations in Task.run (fixes
  Unhandled(Wait) exception from missing effect handler)
- Update all call sites to use run_with_pool instead of bare Lazy.force
- Enable parallel analyzer by default (remove opt-in gate)
- Make apply_params_stack domain-local via Domain.DLS (fixes data race)
- Make alloc_mid use Atomic (consistency with alloc_var)
- Protect DCE CfMaybeUsed writes with field_marker_mutex (fixes flag race)

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* move apply_params_stack to uctx

* kinda works

* add DomainMutex

* Fix DomainMutex data race and thread ID generation race

The DomainMutex.downer field was a non-atomic mutable record field read by
multiple domains without synchronization. In OCaml 5, this is a data race
that can cause a domain to see a stale ownership value, incorrectly thinking
it owns the mutex. Fixed by using Atomic.t for the owner ID field.

Also fixed thread ID generation which used non-atomic Atomic.incr + Atomic.get
pattern. Replaced with atomic Atomic.fetch_and_add.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* pull changes from sub-PR

* investigate if we're actually looking at the problem

* revert

* focus

* focus more

* pull luv changes to get that out of the way

* pull ThreadSafeHashtbl changes because that's a clear bugfix

* pull Deque changes because that's simpler

* use normal mutex for condition because it doesn't have to be reentrant

* enable hanging test again to see how it behaves on other OS

* fix thread id in debug

* don't use Sys.time for Lock.wait

* Initial plan

* Replace Thread.delay with Unix.sleepf in eval to fix hang in Domain-spawned threads

Thread.delay depends on OCaml's threads library per-domain state
(caml_release_domain_lock/caml_acquire_domain_lock), which may not be
properly initialized for domains created via Domain.spawn on Windows
and macOS. Unix.sleepf uses the OCaml runtime's blocking section API
(caml_enter_blocking_section/caml_leave_blocking_section) which works
correctly for any domain.

Also simplified Sys.sleep to avoid mixing OCaml's Sys.time() (CPU time)
with wall-clock sleep durations, and added safety guards for negative
sleep values.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(obs): WebSocketイベント購読方式を実装 (Issue #41)

ポーリングのみだった OBS 連携にイベント購読（EventClient）を追加。

## 変更内容

- ObsController に EventClient（WebSocket イベント購読）を追加
  - 接続時に EventClient への接続を試み、成功すれば即座にイベントを受信
  - on_stream_state_changed / on_current_program_scene_changed を購読
  - EventClient のワーカースレッドが死んだ場合は自動でポーリングに復帰
  - obsws-python 未インストール時はポーリングにフォールバック（従来動作維持）

- ポーリングループの役割変更
  - イベント購読中: 接続死活確認として動作（状態変化はイベントが担う）
  - イベント未接続: 従来通り状態変化を検知（フォールバック）

- poll_once をスレッドセーフに改修
  - 状態比較・更新を _lock で保護（イベント/ポーリングの競合防止）

- using_events プロパティを追加
  - GUI などから現在の接続モードを確認可能

- _ensure_client → _ensure_req_client にリネーム（役割を明確化）

## テスト追加（test_obs_integration.py）
- イベントによる重複通知抑制テスト
- disconnect_event_client の状態クリアテスト
- EventClient コールバックロジックの単体テスト
- using_events / _is_event_client_alive のテスト

* MYTUBE-351 test: automate Test: Firebase auth initialization error — error state displayed

* MYTUBE-351: Fix review issues — remove dead code, replace time.sleep, add README

- Remove _make_blocked_context function (dead code with bug: context.close() before return)
- Replace time.sleep(2) with blocked_page.wait_for_function() polling for alert elements

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* MYTUBE-351: replace network blocking with webpack module interception

Replace context.route() Firebase auth domain blocking with
context.add_init_script webpack module interception.

The network-blocking approach failed in all previous runs because the
Firebase SDK resolves onAuthStateChanged synchronously from
localStorage/IndexedDB for unauthenticated users — no network calls
are made to the blocked domains, so the error callback was never
triggered.

The new approach injects a script before any page scripts run that
intercepts Object.defineProperty to detect when webpack module 9997
exports 'hg' (onAuthStateChanged) and replaces the getter with a
factory returning a fake onAuthStateChanged that always calls the
error callback after 100 ms, directly triggering authError = true in
AuthContext.

README.md updated to document the new simulation method and explain
why network blocking was abandoned.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: signal WS subscribers when TransactionFinalized received from network

When a masternode receives a TransactionFinalized broadcast from a peer,
it was correctly moving the tx to the finalized pool but never calling
signal_tx_finalized(). This meant wallets subscribed to that node would
never receive the utxo_finalized WS event, leaving received transactions
stuck in Pending state indefinitely.

Fix: call consensus.signal_tx_finalized(*txid) after processing the
TransactionFinalized network message, consistent with the other two
finalization paths (local auto-finalization and TimeVote accumulation).

Isolated runner infrastructure for bare-metal Debian 13 servers using
cloud-hypervisor (or QEMU/KVM fallback). Each runner gets its own VM
with separate kernel, Docker daemon, and network stack.

Includes:
- Automated host setup (KVM, networking, storage)
- VM base image builder with cloud-init (Docker, kubectl, doctl, pwsh)
- Supervisor daemon managing 8+ concurrent ephemeral runners
- nftables firewall restricting VM egress to GitHub/DO only
- systemd integration: service, network unit, watchdog timer
- Preflight validation and health checks
- GitHub App authentication for runner registration tokens
- Makefile with full lifecycle management (install/start/stop/enable)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(#56): implement spaced repetition feature with review scheduling

* feat: implement spaced repetition feature with review scheduling

- Add ReviewDuePickerDialog component for selecting review session size.
- Integrate review due functionality in SubjectsPageClient and NotebookCard.
- Create ReviewDueButton for displaying due problems count.
- Implement spaced repetition algorithm in spaced-repetition.ts.
- Update review schedule in the database upon attempts and problem reviews.
- Add API routes for starting spaced review sessions and handling attempts.
- Introduce caching for review schedules and user-specific data.
- Add tests for spaced repetition logic and quality mapping.

* feat: enhance spaced repetition functionality with attempt state caching and session management

* feat: refactor reflection components and implement attempt status management

- Replaced ReflectionDialog with AttemptEditDialog in AttemptTimelineEntry.
- Introduced getStatusBadge function to manage attempt status display.
- Removed ConfidenceSelector and ReflectionDialog components.
- Added AttemptStatusForm for managing attempt status and reflection notes.
- Updated spaced-repetition logic to map problem status to quality scores.
- Modified schemas to include selected_status in attempt data.
- Adjusted tests to reflect changes in status mapping and removed confidence-related tests.

* fix: move render-time setState to useEffect in AttemptStatusForm

Calling setSelectedStatus during render violates React conventions and
risks re-render loops. Move the default status selection logic into a
useEffect with proper dependencies.

* fix: add missing session_type and subject_id to ReviewSessionState type

Spaced repetition sessions use session_type, subject_id, and nullable
problem_set_id, but the TypeScript interface did not model these fields.

* fix: handle upsert errors in updateReviewSchedule

Supabase client returns errors in the response object rather than
throwing, so the callers' try/catch would not catch upsert failures.
Check the error return and throw explicitly. Also use a single
timestamp for last_reviewed_at and updated_at consistency.

* fix: add error toast feedback for failed assessment saves

Replace silent catch blocks in AttemptStatusForm and AttemptEditDialog
with toast.error notifications so users know when a save fails.

* fix: enforce auth-first order and session ownership in SR summary page

The summary page checked sessionId before auth (leaking 404 vs login
state) and had no session ownership validation, allowing any user to
view another user's session summary. Reorder auth check first with
redirect to login, and add user_id + session_type filter matching the
review page pattern.

* style: format code

* fix: prevent double-advancement of SM-2 schedule per review

Remove premature updateReviewSchedule call from the auto-mark attempt
endpoint — the assessment is not finalized until the user confirms via
PATCH. Add a same-day guard in updateReviewSchedule that preserves
SM-2 state (repetition_number, ease_factor, interval_days) when a
problem has already been reviewed today, only refreshing next_review_at.

* fix: validate session_size with Zod in start-spaced endpoint

The session_size field was destructured from the raw body without
validation. Non-numbers, negatives, or floats could produce NaN or
invalid limits passed to the RPC. Add a StartSpacedSessionDto schema
that validates subject_id as UUID and session_size as an optional
integer in [1, 100].

* fix: handle DB errors in active-session lookups for spaced review

Both GET and POST handlers used .single() and ignored the error return,
causing DB/RLS failures to be silently treated as "no active session".
In the POST path this could create duplicate sessions. Switch to
.maybeSingle() (returns null without error for 0 rows) and explicitly
handle real DB errors with 500 responses.

* fix: only sync problem status when editing the latest attempt

Previously, editing any historical attempt's selected_status would
overwrite the problem's current status and recalculate the SR schedule.
Now check if the edited attempt is the most recent for that problem
before syncing status and updating the review schedule.

* fix: invalidate review schedule cache after attempt-level SR updates

Neither POST /api/attempts nor PATCH /api/attempts/[id] called
revalidateUserReviewSchedule after updating the SR schedule, causing
the subject due-count badge to stay stale until TTL expiry or session
completion. Add the call after each successful updateReviewSchedule.

* feat: timezone-aware day boundaries for all date-sensitive features

Make streaks, heatmaps, weekly progress, quotas, and the SM-2 same-day
guard respect the user's configured IANA timezone instead of defaulting
to UTC. Adds auto-detection of browser timezone on login/signup, a
manual timezone selector in profile settings, and sets review due dates
to the user's local midnight so problems are available from the start
of the day.

* fix: enforce max session size in spaced repetition configurations

* fix: preserve assessment input across problems in review sessions

* fix: handle errors when reading review schedule from database

* fix: handle error when syncing problem status in attempt creation

* fix: reduce max requests for problem creation rate limit to 60 per hour

* fix: disable manual timezone input in profile sheet

* feat: redesign SR review button with border trace animation

* chore: update changelog

* chore: update changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: simplify timezone validation in user profile schema

* fix: simplify progress tracking logic in session review

* refactor: remove unused ref for saved attempt ID in AttemptStatusForm

* refactor: optimize date formatting in toMidnightUTC function

* refactor: streamline spaced repetition status update logic in createAttempt function

* refactor: optimize fetching of problem and latest attempt in PATCH function

* refactor: implement useRef for elapsed time tracking in session review components

* style: clean up code formatting

* refactor: update cache tag for user statistics to use new STATISTICS tag

feat: hub-rank scores on illuminate results (1.0=most central → 0)

All illuminate: results previously returned score=1.0 — meaningless to
both humans ('lol all the same') and LLMs (can't prioritize by relevance).

illuminate-global now assigns a normalized hub-rank score:
  score = (N - rank_index) / N
  - score 1.0  → most connected compound in the corpus
  - score ~0   → least connected in the returned set
  - Results sorted descending by score so order matches centrality

Results are also re-sorted by score so the YAML output presents the
most structurally important content first.

UI: score: n.score || 1 → n.score ?? 1 to avoid clobbering a valid
score of 0 for the least-central node.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix workflow run approval by checking all pending statuses and handling PR webhook events (#166)

* Initial plan

* fix: approve workflow runs in action_required, pending, and waiting states

Expand approve_pending_workflow_runs to check all three statuses that
GitHub may use when a workflow run needs approval: action_required (fork
PRs), pending (first-time contributors), and waiting (outside
collaborators / deployment protection rules). For waiting runs, also
attempt to approve via the pending_deployments endpoint.

Add approve_pending_workflow_runs to the worker's GitHubClient and
handle PR opened/ready_for_review/synchronize webhook events to
immediately approve workflows and enable auto-merge instead of waiting
for the next 30-minute CLI cron run.

fix: correctly handle GMS2 tileset borders, tile grid scaling, and global tile dimensions (#6)

* fix(extract): resolve GMS2 compat layer tile tilesets via SpriteDefinition

In GMS2 (Deltarune), tiles in Compatibility_Tiles layers have a null
BackgroundDefinition. The tileset reference is stored in SpriteDefinition
instead, pointing to a UndertaleSprite named 'bg_*'.

Also export all sprites with 'bg_' prefix into backgrounds.json so the
Rust conversion can find their texture data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(gms2): use GMS2TileWidth/Height for native tile layer tilesets

GMS2 Background resources store explicit tile dimensions (GMS2TileWidth /
GMS2TileHeight). Previously determine_tile_dims() divided the tileset
texture size by the room's tile count (tiles_x / tiles_y), producing
wrong values (e.g. 12x34 instead of 20x20 for bg_towntiles_tileset).

Changes:
- extract_data.csx: export gms2TileWidth/gms2TileHeight for Data.Backgrounds
- schema.rs: add gms2_tile_width/gms2_tile_height fields to BackgroundDef
- convert.rs: use these fields directly when non-zero, skipping the
  broken tile-count division heuristic

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace dynamic imports with static imports to fix asset pipeline

The webview message handlers in useExtensionMessages.ts used dynamic
import() calls (e.g. import('../office/sprites/assetLoader.js').then(...))
to lazily load asset-management modules. Vite code-splits these into
separate chunks whose module-level state is isolated from the main
bundle. For example, calling setWallSprites on the chunk's copy of
wallTiles.ts had NO effect on the copy used by renderer.ts (imported
statically). This caused ALL three rendering pipelines to silently fail:

1. Character sprite sheets — loadCharacterSheetsFromUris stored sheets
   in the chunk's characterSheets Map, not the renderer's copy
2. Tileset metadata — setTilesetMetadata stored the PNG image in the
   chunk's tilesetMetadataImage, so drawTilesetTile always returned
   false (plain beige/tan floor fallback)
3. Legacy tileset data — setLegacyTilesetAssets wrote to the chunk's
   tilesetData, so drawTilesetFurniture always returned false (gray
   rectangle furniture fallback)

Changes:
- Convert all dynamic import() to static import in useExtensionMessages
- Add try/catch around removeBackground() in character sheet loading
- Add connect-src to CSP so fetch()-based asset loading can work
- Set Vite emptyOutDir: true to prevent stale code-split chunks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
